author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: it would be interesting if one could design a maglev roto'r's topography to compensate for epicycles so as to bypass Earnshaw's theorem for a permanently (firmamentally) stable-in-place maglev spin with a non-mechanically-touching bearing component so that the topology / topography's compensatory design would bypass the need for a mechanical bearing, whereas the bearing and rotor are one and the same maglev component reliant on the topology for aforesaid properties, and composite in design. I'm not sure if I am making sense.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: go for it

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: i wonder if those Aztecs and shroom-chomping Maya were heliocentric. They did have that "sun-god" Kukulkan to whom they sacrificed those chums'hearts to on that 'pyramid in Teonochtitlan. Interestingly shrooms are called "teonanacatl"and I always wonder what the etymology is as teonanacatl refers to "flesh of the gods"" (shrooms, the seratonin analog / isomer). I wonder what the morpheme teo means in Nahuatl or if it is in fact a borrowed morpheme from the conquistadors! Not many can answer that, I haven'found an answer.I find it curiosu the Chinese term "Dao (The way) has such religious significance as Deus / deus pater (Jupiter).

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate -never really thought about it - thanks for taking the time to post

replies.author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I'm no good when I have red wine, my apologies.

author: @minimoa69
text:
I'm stunned that you've done this in three days - that's an incredible bit of work!! Have you included the spiral dial side for the eclipse prediction, too, or just the planetarium? I couldn't quite tell from the video.
I've actually built 11 of these since 2016 from wood, but mine were all hand cut, and each took a little longer than three days! I really hope that you now go the extra mile and finish this off with dials, graduations, etc, on both sides, and then do a proper calibration to a specific modern date so that it will truly show planet positions, eclipses and so on. You've done the complex bit, after all!! Brilliant job, anyhow, and I'll be thinking of you when i next hand cut another set of 52 gears for one of mine! 👏😁

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I had to go and look - your work is truly beautiful. I only did the planetarium bit. I am so impressed by what you did and somewhat humbled - you are craftsman!

author: @ΜικεΡ
text:
Laser cutters are so cool

Apologies if you've already done this and I missed it, but you should make a 'deconstructed' cycloidal drive out of atleast 3 2D profiles stacked ontop of each other (instead of a single swept/3D profile). 
Its probably not video-worthy in itself, but it in my mind it kindof reinforces that the cycloidal drive is simply a gear train, even though they look nothing like involute gears.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good idea mate thanks for suggesting it

author: @jamesross1003
text: A future punk build of an ancient machine.  Got to love it.  Beautiful job Robert!  Bit of a steam punk guy myself, but hey add a brass compass and brass sundial and voila you have the best of all the punks.  You have the future punk tech vibe going on, a brass compass for steam punk, and a sundial to give a Victorian punk vibe and it would have it all.  Oh man there is so much going on with this build and I love it.  It is not simply a machine it is a work of art that can and in my opinion should continue to evolve.  Thanks so much for this Robert!  Not having a particularly good day, but this put a smile on my face.  It is a much needed bright spot in my day.  Keep doing what you are doing.  Thanks again.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you couild really 'ounk' this up mate - now that's fun!

replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Yeah there is so much that could be done to really punk it up.  It's already a functional piece of art.  I can't see any downsides.   Really liking this build.  So many possibilities.  Thanks Robert!
